name: engrama
description: Graph-based long-term memory for AI agents. Engrama stores entities, observations and relationships as a knowledge graph and lets agents traverse it to reconstruct context on demand. SQLite by default (zero external services); Neo4j optional for multi-process production.

NEO4J_URI.description: Bolt URI of the Neo4j server (neo4j backend), e.g. bolt://localhost:7687.

NEO4J_PASSWORD.description: Neo4j password (neo4j backend). Provide via environment/secret store, never commit.

VAULT_PATH.description: Absolute path to Engrama's Obsidian vault; enables the sync/ingest tools when set.

ENGRAMA_REQUIRE_IDENTITY.description: Fail-closed multi-tenant mode: reject requests with no X-Engrama-Org-Id / X-Engrama-User-Id headers instead of pooling them into the standalone identity.

tools.name: engrama_status
tools.title: Engrama Status
tools.description: Return a snapshot of the running Engrama MCP server's configuration.

tools.name: engrama_search
tools.title: Search Memory Graph
tools.description:
Search the memory graph.  Use this at the START of every session
to load context relevant to the current topic, and whenever you need
to check whether a node already exists before creating it.

tools.name: engrama_remember
tools.title: Remember (Merge Node)
tools.description: Store a piece of knowledge as a node in the memory graph.

label.description: Node label — one of: CTF, Client, Concept, Course, Dataset, Decision, Domain, Exercise, Experiment, Gear, Insight, Location, Material, Model, Person, Photo, Pipeline, Problem, Project, Species, Target, Technique, Technology, Tool, Vulnerability.
label.title: Label

properties.description:
Node properties. Must include 'name' (or 'title' for Decision/Problem). For rich retrieval, include enrichment fields:
  - 'summary': 2–3 sentence overview — what this is, why it matters
  - 'details': comprehensive context — techniques, decisions, approaches, alternatives, key examples. The richer this field, the more useful the memory becomes.
  - 'tags': freeform list for filtering, e.g. ["active-directory", "credential-access", "windows"]
  - 'source': how this knowledge was captured ("conversation", "ingest", "manual", "sync")
  - 'status': current state ("active", "resolved", "superseded")
Example of a GOOD node:
{"name": "kerberoasting-lab", "summary": "Hands-on lab demonstrating Kerberoasting against AD service accounts using Rubeus and Hashcat, with detection via Event 4769.", "details": "Students request TGS tickets for SPNs with Rubeus kerberoast, crack offline with Hashcat. Covers RC4 vs AES crackability, detection via Event 4769 anomalies. Defense: gMSA, AES-only policy, honey tokens.", "tags": ["active-directory", "credential-access", "lab"], "source": "conversation"}
Example of a BAD (too thin) node: {"name": "kerberoasting-lab", "description": "Lab about kerberoasting"}
properties.title: Properties

relations.description: Optional relations to create in the same call. Format: {"REL_TYPE": ["target_name", ...]}. Example: {"USES": ["BDK"], "IN_DOMAIN": ["teaching"], "FOR": ["Accenture"]}. Targets are matched by name; a missing target is created as a stub. By default the stub's label is inferred from the relation type, which is lossy. To pin it, pass an object instead of a string: {"RELATED_TO": [{"name": "BDK", "label": "Tool"}]}.
relations.title: Relations

tools.name: engrama_relate
tools.title: Relate (Merge Relationship)
tools.description:
Connect two nodes with a typed relationship.  Always call this
right after engrama_remember to wire the new node into the graph.
Also use it to record newly discovered connections between existing
nodes.  Both endpoints must already exist (create them first with
engrama_remember if needed).

from_label.description: Label of the source node — one of: CTF, Client, Concept, Course, Dataset, Decision, Domain, Exercise, Experiment, Gear, Insight, Location, Material, Model, Person, Photo, Pipeline, Problem, Project, Species, Target, Technique, Technology, Tool, Vulnerability.
from_label.title: From Label

rel_type.description: Relationship type — one of: APPLIES, BELONGS_TO, CAUSED_BY, COMPOSED_OF, CONTRADICTS, COVERS, DEPENDS_ON, DOCUMENTS, EVALUATES, EXECUTED_WITH, EXPLOITS, FEATURES, FEEDS, FOR, HAS, HAS_MATERIAL, IMPLEMENTS, INCLUDES, INFORMED_BY, INHABITS, INSTANCE_OF, INVOLVES, IN_DOMAIN, LINKS_TO, ORIGIN_OF, PERFORMS, PRACTICES, PREREQUISITE_OF, RELATED_TO, REPLACES, REQUIRES, RUNS, SERVES, SHOT_WITH, SIMILAR_TO, SOLVED_BY, SUBSET_OF, TAKEN_AT, TARGETS, TEACHES, TRAINS_ON, USES.
rel_type.title: Rel Type

to_label.description: Label of the target node — one of: CTF, Client, Concept, Course, Dataset, Decision, Domain, Exercise, Experiment, Gear, Insight, Location, Material, Model, Person, Photo, Pipeline, Problem, Project, Species, Target, Technique, Technology, Tool, Vulnerability.
to_label.title: To Label

tools.name: engrama_context
tools.title: Get Context (Neighbourhood)
tools.description:
Retrieve a node and its neighbourhood up to N hops.  Use this to
build rich context before answering a question — e.g. fetch the
user's Person node to see their projects, or a Technology node to
see what it connects to.  Works even for isolated nodes (returns the
node with an empty neighbours list).

label.description: Label of the starting node — one of: CTF, Client, Concept, Course, Dataset, Decision, Domain, Exercise, Experiment, Gear, Insight, Location, Material, Model, Person, Photo, Pipeline, Problem, Project, Species, Target, Technique, Technology, Tool, Vulnerability.
label.title: Label

tools.name: engrama_sync_note
tools.title: Sync Single Note to Graph
tools.description: Sync a single note from Engrama's internal Obsidian vault to the graph.

path.description: Vault-relative path to the note, e.g. '10-projects/engrama.md'.
path.title: Path

dry_run.description: When true, do not write to the graph or inject `engrama_id` into the note's frontmatter. Return the same JSON shape as a real sync, with a `would_*` view of what the operation would have done. Useful to preview a sync against a different vault before committing.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

tools.name: engrama_sync_vault
tools.title: Sync Entire Vault to Graph
tools.description: Scan Engrama's internal Obsidian vault and reconcile all notes.

folder.description: Optional folder to restrict the scan (vault-relative). Empty string scans the entire vault.
folder.title: Folder

dry_run.description: When true, do not write to the graph or inject `engrama_id` into any note's frontmatter. Return the same JSON shape as a real sync, with counts of what *would* be created/updated/skipped and the list of files that would receive an `engrama_id` injection. Useful to preview the impact before committing to a full scan.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

tools.name: engrama_ingest
tools.title: Ingest Content
tools.description:
Read a document, conversation, or text and return its content with
entity extraction guidance.  After calling this tool, use the returned
content and extraction prompt to identify entities and relationships,
then call ``engrama_remember`` with inline relations for each entity.

source.description: Content to ingest. Either a vault-relative path to a note (e.g. '10-projects/websocket-debugging.md') or raw text content.
source.title: Source

source_type.description: Type of source: 'note' (vault path — will be read), 'text' (raw text content), or 'conversation' (conversation transcript).
source_type.title: Source Type

context_hint.description: Optional hint about the context (e.g. project name, domain, course). Helps guide entity extraction.
context_hint.title: Context Hint

tools.name: engrama_reflect
tools.title: Reflect (Adaptive Pattern Detection)
tools.description:
Detect cross-entity patterns in the memory graph.  Call this
periodically (e.g. at the end of a session or when the user asks
for insights) to discover solution transfers between projects,
shared technologies, training opportunities, technique transfers,
concept clusters, stale knowledge, and under-connected nodes.

tools.name: engrama_surface_insights
tools.title: Surface Pending Insights
tools.description:
Retrieve pending Insights for human review.  Call this after
engrama_reflect, or periodically, to show the user patterns the
graph has detected.  Present each Insight and ask the user to
approve or dismiss it — never act on an Insight without explicit
approval.

limit.description: Maximum number of pending Insights to return.
limit.title: Limit

title.description: Optional: return only the pending Insight with this exact title.
title.title: Title

query.description: Optional: return only pending Insights semantically related to this query (same cosine gate as the search proactive hint). Ignored if ``title`` is given.
query.title: Query

tools.name: engrama_approve_insight
tools.title: Approve or Dismiss Insight
tools.description: Approve or dismiss a pending Insight after human review.

tools.name: engrama_write_insight_to_vault
tools.title: Write Approved Insight to Note
tools.description: Append an approved Insight as a section in an Obsidian note.

title.description: Exact title of the approved Insight.
title.title: Title

target_note.description: Relative path to the Obsidian note to append to.
target_note.title: Target Note

tools.name: engrama_reindex
tools.title: Reindex Embeddings
tools.description: Find and repair nodes that are missing their vector embedding.

mode.description: One of 'detect' | 'classify' | 'apply'. Run them in that order.
mode.title: Mode

dry_run.description: Only meaningful for mode='apply'. Default true (simulate). Pass false explicitly to actually re-embed and write.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

limit.description: Maximum number of vector-less nodes to process in this call.
limit.title: Limit

tools.name: engrama_gdpr_forget
tools.title: Erase My Data (GDPR right-to-erasure)
tools.description: Permanently erase **the caller's own** memory (GDPR, Spec 001 US-3).

mode.description: 'dry-run' (default, safe) reports what would be erased without deleting anything; 'apply' permanently erases the caller's own identity. There is no undo and no server-side backup.
mode.title: Mode
